channel.title: Lme4 on Lisa DeBruine
channel.description: Recent content in Lme4 on Lisa DeBruine

item.title: Simulating Mixed Effects
item.description: [Updated 2019-01-03] library(tidyverse) library(lmerTest) I’m going to walk through one example of simulating a dataset with random effects. I’ll generate data for a study where subjects are in one of two conditions (A and B) and respond to stimuli shown in each of two versions (congruent and incongruent). The DV is reaction time. Subjects First, we need to generate a sample of subjects. Each subject will have slightly faster or slower reaction times on average; this is their random intercept (sub_i).
